questions.question: What is a deadlock?
options: A) A situation where a process is waiting for a resource
B) A situation where a resource is waiting for a process
C) A situation where two or more processes are blocked indefinitely
D) A situation where a process is running faster than other processes
questions.explanation: A situation where two or more processes are blocked indefinitely, each waiting for the other to release a resource.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a deadlock?
options: A) Process P1 is waiting for resource R2, while Process P2 is waiting for resource R1
B) Process P1 is running on CPU, while Process P2 is waiting for I/O
C) Process P1 is waiting for resource R1, while Process P2 is waiting for resource R2
D) Process P1 is waiting for disk I/O, while Process P2 is waiting for network I/O
questions.explanation: If two or more processes are blocked indefinitely, each waiting for the other to release a resource, then it is a deadlock.

questions.question: What is a necessary condition for a deadlock to occur?
options: A) Both processes must be running on the same CPU
B) Both processes must be waiting for the same resource
C) Both processes must be blocked indefinitely, each waiting for the other to release a resource
D) Both processes must be running faster than other processes
questions.explanation: A necessary condition for a deadlock to occur is that both processes are blocked indefinitely, each waiting for the other to release a resource.

questions.question: Which of the following methods can be used to handle deadlocks?
options: A) Terminate one of the processes involved in the deadlock
B) Release one of the resources held by the processes involved in the deadlock
C) Both A and B
D) Neither A nor B
questions.explanation: Both terminating one of the processes involved in the deadlock and releasing one of the resources held by the processes involved in the deadlock can be used to handle deadlocks.

questions.question: What is a deadlock-free algorithm?
options: A) An algorithm that always acquires resources in the same order
B) An algorithm that never acquires resources that are held by other processes
C) An algorithm that always releases resources in the same order
D) An algorithm that never locks any resources
questions.explanation: A deadlock-free algorithm is one that never acquires resources that are held by other processes, reducing the likelihood of deadlock.
